metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: The way consumers search for products is changing drastically. Previously, Google was the gateway to the internet, but today we are seeing a strong shift towards AI assistants. These tools formulate answers themselves, make recommendations and even direct purchases. For retailers, this means less control over the customer journey, less website traffic and greater dependence on the data that AI uses to make choices. This blog describes what this development means in concrete terms and how a retailer can respond to it.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: Previously, Google was the gateway to the internet, but today we are seeing a strong shift towards AI assistants. These tools formulate answers themselves, make recommendations and even direct purchases. For retailers, this means less control over the customer journey, less website traffic and greater dependence on the data that AI uses to make choices. This blog describes what this development means in concrete terms and how a retailer can respond to it. The changing customer journey: from clicks to answers.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: These tools formulate answers themselves, make recommendations and even direct purchases. For retailers, this means less control over the customer journey, less website traffic and greater dependence on the data that AI uses to make choices. This blog describes what this development means in concrete terms and how a retailer can respond to it. The changing customer journey: from clicks to answers. To date, consumers shopping online started with a Google search, followed by clicking on a few websites, comparing products and hopefully making a purchase.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: For retailers, this means less control over the customer journey, less website traffic and greater dependence on the data that AI uses to make choices. This blog describes what this development means in concrete terms and how a retailer can respond to it. The changing customer journey: from clicks to answers. To date, consumers shopping online started with a Google search, followed by clicking on a few websites, comparing products and hopefully making a purchase. AI tools such as Google AI Overview, ChatGPT, and Perplexity largely take this work off the hands of the consumer. > “The AI ​​assistant then provides ready-made advice about the best options, based on price, quality, customer reviews, quality marks and available data, without the consumer having to visit websites themselves.”.

Henk Hofstede.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: AI tools such as Google AI Overview, ChatGPT, and Perplexity largely take this work off the hands of the consumer. > “The AI ​​assistant then provides ready-made advice about the best options, based on price, quality, customer reviews, quality marks and available data, without the consumer having to visit websites themselves.”. Henk Hofstede. Sector Banker Retail. Suppose a consumer is looking for a home sound system. Where he used to look for a 'home sound system', he now searches via AI assistants for a 'sound system for the entire house, with a living area of ​​150 m², spread over four rooms, with a simple installation and a maximum budget of 2,000 euros'.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: Suppose a consumer is looking for a home sound system. Where he used to look for a 'home sound system', he now searches via AI assistants for a 'sound system for the entire house, with a living area of ​​150 m², spread over four rooms, with a simple installation and a maximum budget of 2,000 euros'. The AI ​​assistant then provides ready-made advice about the best options, based on price, quality, customer reviews, quality marks and available data, without the consumer having to visit websites themselves. This has major consequences for the retailer. Direct website visits decrease because the AI ​​already presents the information to the consumer.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: Where he used to look for a 'home sound system', he now searches via AI assistants for a 'sound system for the entire house, with a living area of ​​150 m², spread over four rooms, with a simple installation and a maximum budget of 2,000 euros'. The AI ​​assistant then provides ready-made advice about the best options, based on price, quality, customer reviews, quality marks and available data, without the consumer having to visit websites themselves. This has major consequences for the retailer. Direct website visits decrease because the AI ​​already presents the information to the consumer. In addition, the retailer loses influence on the customer journey and brand experience, because the AI ​​determines which product, brand or store is shown.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: The AI ​​assistant then provides ready-made advice about the best options, based on price, quality, customer reviews, quality marks and available data, without the consumer having to visit websites themselves. This has major consequences for the retailer. Direct website visits decrease because the AI ​​already presents the information to the consumer. In addition, the retailer loses influence on the customer journey and brand experience, because the AI ​​determines which product, brand or store is shown. The competition is increasing because the retailer is competing with all products that objectively score better on the desired criteria based on the available data.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: The competition is increasing because the retailer is competing with all products that objectively score better on the desired criteria based on the available data. AI determines what the consumer sees. AI assistants mainly use information that is freely accessible on the internet, such as web pages, product descriptions, reviews and ratings. Sometimes it concerns plain text, sometimes it concerns structured product information that is specially designed so that AI systems can easily read it. Some AI systems obtain information from public product and quality mark databases. > “In short, AI relies on what is publicly discoverable, structured and credible.”.

Risks for the retailer The rise of AI-based search engines leads to a number of concrete risks for retailers, such as the risk of invisibility where products are not mentioned if the data is incorrect or does not meet AI standards.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: Does the retailer not have its product information in order? Then there is a good chance that AI will simply skip its products. Risks for the retailer. Risks for the retailer The rise of AI-based search engines leads to a number of concrete risks for retailers, such as the risk of invisibility where products are not mentioned if the data is incorrect or does not meet AI standards. Price pressure is also a risk if AI only selects for the lowest price, leaving out a brand with more quality but a higher price.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: Then there is a good chance that AI will simply skip its products. Risks for the retailer. Risks for the retailer The rise of AI-based search engines leads to a number of concrete risks for retailers, such as the risk of invisibility where products are not mentioned if the data is incorrect or does not meet AI standards. Price pressure is also a risk if AI only selects for the lowest price, leaving out a brand with more quality but a higher price. In addition, the retailer will miss out on crucial customer data if the purchase by the AI ​​assistant is not made via the retailer but via an external platform such as Zalando or Amazon if they have better findable data.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: Risks for the retailer. Risks for the retailer The rise of AI-based search engines leads to a number of concrete risks for retailers, such as the risk of invisibility where products are not mentioned if the data is incorrect or does not meet AI standards. Price pressure is also a risk if AI only selects for the lowest price, leaving out a brand with more quality but a higher price. In addition, the retailer will miss out on crucial customer data if the purchase by the AI ​​assistant is not made via the retailer but via an external platform such as Zalando or Amazon if they have better findable data. This is also called zero-click commerce.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text: Risks for the retailer The rise of AI-based search engines leads to a number of concrete risks for retailers, such as the risk of invisibility where products are not mentioned if the data is incorrect or does not meet AI standards. Price pressure is also a risk if AI only selects for the lowest price, leaving out a brand with more quality but a higher price. In addition, the retailer will miss out on crucial customer data if the purchase by the AI ​​assistant is not made via the retailer but via an external platform such as Zalando or Amazon if they have better findable data. This is also called zero-click commerce. Finally, there is a danger of fragmentation of the brand experience, because AI determines how it is presented and, for example, makes neutral, price and/or sustainable recommendations, which reduces customer loyalty. > “Finally, there is a danger of fragmentation of the brand experience, because AI determines how it is presented.”.

Henk Hofstede.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text:
Henk Hofstede. Sector Banker Retail
Promising measures for the retailer. Yet there are smart ways retailers can arm themselves against this AI revolution to maintain visibility and control:. 1. Write extensive, unique and complete product content - This is the basics and can be in the form of text or images. This includes well-structured product data on the website, with detailed descriptions, specifications and important certifications, but also how and why the product is used.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

text:
Sector Banker Retail
Promising measures for the retailer. Yet there are smart ways retailers can arm themselves against this AI revolution to maintain visibility and control:. 1. Write extensive, unique and complete product content - This is the basics and can be in the form of text or images. This includes well-structured product data on the website, with detailed descriptions, specifications and important certifications, but also how and why the product is used. This must demonstrate that the retailer is a 'product expert'.

metadata.source_title: How retailers can lose or maintain their visibility due to AI

Yet there are smart ways retailers can arm themselves against this AI revolution to maintain visibility and control:. 1. Write extensive, unique and complete product content - This is the basics and can be in the form of text or images. This includes well-structured product data on the website, with detailed descriptions, specifications and important certifications, but also how and why the product is used. This must demonstrate that the retailer is a 'product expert'. The AI ​​assistant links the user question to the information on a website or product page and sees this as the source of the answer.

5. Focus on brand preference and community - AI assistants make rational choices, but people also make emotional choices and not all purchases will go through AI assistants. Therefore, invest in strong brand stories and transparency about sustainability, origin and quality of the product. By doing community building, building relationships through social media or loyalty programs, the retailer can remain distinctive and maintain a bond with those fans who continue to come to him directly.
